payload.answer: Store the table externally or as a structured attachment; do not memorize all 500 rows verbatim. If anything is retained in Brain, keep only a compact summary/schema-level fact unless a specific row-level fact is explicitly durable and relevant.
payload.decision: Do not commit the 500-row CSV verbatim as durable memory. Prefer storing it as a structured external artifact/reference, and if any memory is needed, retain only a compact schema-level summary or other durable facts explicitly relevant to future use.

entity_resolution.reason: No persistent real-world entity was introduced; the input is a generic data artifact.

payload.intent: Determine how a large repetitive CSV table (500 rows of id,name,value) should be stored in Brain.

relationships.object: structured reference rather than verbatim durable memory
relationships.subject: CSV table with 500 rows

memory_cards.statement: A large repetitive CSV table should not be stored as raw row-level durable memory; keep it as a structured reference or file and only extract stable, high-level facts if they are worth remembering.

details: Parsed policy question about storage of a large repetitive CSV table.
Avoided committing row-level contents as durable memory.

repair_options: If the table must be searchable later, index it as structured data instead of memory cards.
If a subset of rows is important, extract only those specific durable facts into separate cards.
If the table is temporary, omit durable memory entirely and keep only a reference to the source artifact.
